title: Month-end seeding can create duplicate current-month contributions
reasoning: Carbon's default month overflow semantics mean dates like 2026-05-31 subtract one month to 2026-05-01, not 2026-04-30. On those dates, the seeder treats the previous contribution and current contribution as the same year/month for every member. That either creates corrupt demo data or trips the contribution uniqueness constraint for user/month.
reproduction: Freeze time to 2026-05-31 and run FamilyMemberSeeder on an empty migrated database. The first member gets a May previous contribution, then the current contribution also targets May.
recommendation: Compute the previous period without month overflow, for example from the first day of the current month or with subMonthNoOverflow(), and use fresh immutable/copy instances for due_date and paid_at values.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for FamilyMemberSeeder. The existing ProductionSeederTest exercises ProductionSeeder only and does not freeze time around month-end dates.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Pest test that travels to 2026-05-31, runs FamilyMemberSeeder, and asserts each paying member has one previous-month contribution for April 2026 and one current-month contribution for May 2026 with no duplicate-key failure.
minimumFixScope: Update FamilyMemberSeeder previous-month date calculation and add one date-boundary seeder test.
